content.rendered:
This may be the first Springsteen cover to feature the nyckelharpa (at least the first one that I’m aware of), and it’s beautiful.

Alexander and Willms and Stefan Antheck collaborate on a series of wonderful covers of classic songs (not just Bruce’s, although they cover a few of his). This is my favorite though–their cover of “Something in the Night” perfectly preserves the song’s lonely, late night ambiance while bringing something fresh and new to it.

Take a listen below.

© September 3, 2019

excerpt.rendered: German duo UnTechWHV turns in a beautifully atmospheric cover of “Something in the Night.” Who knew the song was so perfect for the nyckelharpa?

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Elysium: Drive All Night
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Irish cover band Elysium breathes new life into "Drive All Night" with their achingly beautiful mash-up.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Darlene Love: Night Closing In
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: When Steve Van Zandt produced a comeback album for Darlene Love, Bruce contributed an original song that's both unmistakably Springsteenian and custom-tailored for Love. Take a listen inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Iacopo Fedi: Man’s Job
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: At the Cover Me finals in Bergamo, the surprise of the night was Iacopo Fedi's powerful, acoustic reinvention of "Man's Job."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Precious Metal: Two Hearts
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Check out Precious Metal's hard-driving, power pop rendition of "Two Hearts," approved by The Boss himself (or so we're told).

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Stayin’ Alive
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The Bee Gees' 1978 hit single inspired once of Bruce's best and most unexpected covers ever.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Jason Flynn: Drive All Night
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Every once in a while, a beautiful Springsteen cover comes out of nowhere. Here's one, from a busker on the streets of Dublin.
